propositions: Removed env vars and TOML keys are silently ignored or defaulted, redirecting the store or weakening policy without any signal to the operator.

A RemovedInput tombstone registry raises a dedicated RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException so it cannot be swallowed by broad except ConfigError/except Exception handlers in tracker_dir and MCP boot.

Error-class retired inputs affecting store location, write/sync gates, auth, security, or lifecycle policy must fail with nonzero exit naming old name, replacement, and removed-in version, while operationally-inert inputs only warn.

Tombstone checks must be placed before the config result-cache lookup (and at the single env-read source tracker_dir_override) so they fire on warm cache hits across CLI, LLM, and long-running MCP server paths.
Genuinely-unknown keys retain the existing forward-compatibility policy (warn by default, error only under REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS=error).

A CI golden-file anti-regression guard fails if a recognised config key or env var is removed without a corresponding tombstone or alias entry, and a config validate/doctor command reports all matches without raising.
